description:
## Overview

Start and stop RFID tag reading operations on the device. During active inventory, the device continuously reads tags and publishes tag data events.

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `control_operation` | Start or stop RFID inventory scanning operations |
| `start_inventory` | Initiate RFID tag reading based on current configuration |

## Behavior

While running, the device continuously reads RFID tags and publishes tag data events. Operating mode and post-filter settings affect read behavior.

post.description:
The control_operation command configures or updates the active radio or scanner operation state on the reader.

This command allows you to configure:
- Control type selection (RFID or SCANNER)
- Start or stop operation for the selected control type
- Real-time radio activity state management
- Inventory operation lifecycle control

Use this command to:
- Start RFID inventory operations on demand
- Stop active radio or scanner operations
- Manage device activity state in real-time

### Command Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern Name | RFID Operation Control |
| Communication Type | Bidirectional (Cloud to Device, Device to Cloud) |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |
| Related Commands | [get_operating_mode](#op-get-operating-mode), [set_operating_mode](#op-set-operating-mode), [get_status](#op-get-status) |
| Required Request Fields | command, requestId, ctrlOprPayload |
| Supported Operations | START, STOP |
| Supported Types | Control types: RFID, SCANNER |
| Supported Profiles | Not applicable |
| Supported Protocols | Not applicable |
| Supported API Versions | V1.0, V1.1 |

command.description: Specifies the name of the command to be executed. In this case, it is control_operation.

requestId.description: A unique identifier for the request. This ensures traceability and prevents duplicate processing of the same request.

ctrlOprPayload.title: ctrlOprPayload
ctrlOprPayload.description: Object that defines what to control and which action to perform. Typical values include control type RFID and operation START or STOP.

controlType.description: Selects which reader subsystem to control.

operation.description: Action to execute for the selected control type. Use START to begin the operation and STOP to end it.

command.description: Echoes the command name so clients can map this response to the control_operation request.

requestId.description: Request identifier copied from the command, used for request and response correlation.

apiVersion.description: API version used to interpret this response payload.

response.title: response
response.description: Execution result object containing status code and result description.

code.description:
Response code indicating success or failure.

- 0 — Success
- 11 — Inventory in progress
- 12 — No Radio Operation in Progress
- 23 — Invalid enum value

description.description: Human-readable result message that explains the response code.

x-error-codes.cause: An RFID inventory operation is currently running
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Stop the current inventory before starting a new operation or changing the mode

x-error-codes.cause: A stop command was sent but no radio operation is currently active
x-error-codes.recommended_action: No action required — the radio is already idle

x-error-codes.cause: A field value does not match any of the allowed enum options
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the schema for allowed values and correct the field
